docs.title: DFVG - DJI Footage Variant Generator
docs.text: DFVG is a professional-grade transcoding and proxy generation tool designed specifically for DJI action cameras (Action 2, Action 3, Action 4, Action 5 Pro). It automates the ingestion, transcoding, and proxy generation workflow, ensuring your footage is ready for editing immediately.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Automated Ingestion: Scans directories for DJI footage.
  • Transcoding: Creates high-quality ProRes 422 HQ intermediates.
  • Proxy Generation: Generates lightweight H.265 proxies for smooth editing.
  • Metadata Preservation: Retains original timecodes and metadata.
  • Multi-Platform:
    • CLI: Powerful command-line interface for scripting.
    • Web UI: Modern React-based dashboard for local network access.
    • Desktop App: Electron-based application for macOS.
    • Mobile App: React Native app for remote monitoring and control.
  • Network Serving: Bind to 0.0.0.0 for LAN access and control via mobile app.

docs.title: Understanding Modes
docs.text: DFVG is designed around two primary workflows for DJI footage:

docs.title: 1. Compact Mode (H.265)
docs.text: Best for: Quick previewing, mobile transfer, and storage efficiency. - Proxies: Generates small, high-efficiency H.265 proxies (720p). - Master: Relinks to original source files. - Use Case: You just shot a lot of footage and want to view it on your phone or laptop without filling up space.

docs.title: 2. ProRes Mode (HQ)
docs.text: Best for: Professional editing in DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, or Final Cut. - Intermediates: Transcodes source footage to ProRes 422 HQ. This codec is "visually lossless" and scrubs smoothly in editors, unlike H.265. - Proxies: Also generates H.265 proxies for quick reference. - Use Case: You are starting a serious edit and want the best possible performance and color grading flexibility.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install dfvg

docs.title: Basic Usage (CLI)
docs.text: dfvg process /path/to/footage

docs.title: Launch Web Interface
docs.text: dfvg-web

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: The DFVG API provides endpoints for external applications to trigger transcoding, check job status, and manage the system.

docs.title: POST /scan
docs.text:
Scans a directory for DJI footage and returns a list of detected clips.

Parameters:

  • path: The directory path to scan.

Response:

{
  "clips": [
    {
      "filename": "DJI_001.MP4",
      "path": "/Volumes/SD_CARD/DCIM/100MEDIA/DJI_001.MP4",
      "size": 123456789,
      "metadata": {
        "width": 3840,
        "height": 2160,
        "fps": 60,
        "codec": "hevc"
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: POST /jobs
docs.text:
Starts a transcoding job for a specific file.

Parameters:

  • path: The path to the file to transcode.
  • mode: The transcoding mode (A for Compact, B for ProRes).

Response:

{
  "job_id": "12345",
  "status": "pending"
}

docs.title: GET /jobs/{id}
docs.text:
Retrieves the status of a specific job.

Parameters:

  • id: The ID of the job to retrieve.

Response:

{
  "job_id": "12345",
  "status": "processing",
  "progress": 50,
  "current_file": "DJI_001.MP4",
  "message": "Transcoding..."
}

docs.title: GET /system
docs.text:
Retrieves system status information.

Response:

{
  "cpu_usage": 10,
  "disk_usage": 50,
  "active_jobs": 1
}

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text: The DFVG Command Line Interface (CLI) provides a powerful way to interact with the transcoding engine, suitable for automation and advanced workflows.

docs.title: Basic Syntax
docs.text:
dfvg process [OPTIONS] [PATH]
  • PATH: The directory containing the footage to process. Defaults to the current directory if not specified.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --mode [A|B]: Select processing mode.

    • A: Compact (H.265 proxies only)
    • B: ProRes 422 HQ (High Quality intermediate + H.265 proxy)
    • Default: A

  • --output [PATH]: Specify a custom output directory. If not provided, output will be saved alongside the original footage in a proxies or transcoded subdirectory.

  • --threads [N]: Number of threads to use for transcoding. Defaults to auto (CPU count - 1).

  • --verbose, -v: Enable verbose logging.

  • --help: Show help message.

docs.title: Process Current Directory (Compact Mode)
docs.text: dfvg process

docs.title: Process Specific Folder (ProRes Mode)
docs.text: dfvg process --mode B /Volumes/SD_CARD/DCIM/100MEDIA

docs.title: Specify Output Directory
docs.text: dfvg process --output /Users/user/Movies/Project1 /Volumes/SD_CARD/DCIM/100MEDIA

docs.title: Batch Processing
docs.text:
You can process multiple directories by passing them as arguments:

dfvg process /path/to/folder1 /path/to/folder2

docs.title: Monitoring Progress
docs.text: The CLI displays a progress bar for each file being processed, showing the percentage complete, current file name, and estimated remaining time.

docs.title: Developer Guide
docs.text: This guide provides information for developers who want to contribute to DFVG or understand its internal architecture.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
DFVG is built with a modular architecture:

  • CLI: The main entry point for command-line operations.
  • Transcoding Engine: Core logic for handling file ingestion, transcoding, and proxy generation.
  • Web Interface: A React-based frontend served by a FastAPI backend.
  • Mobile App: A React Native app for remote monitoring and control.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
• dfvg/: Core Python package.
    • cli.py: Command-line interface logic.
    • transcode.py: Transcoding logic.
    • api/: FastAPI backend.
  • frontend/: React frontend code.
  • mobile/: React Native mobile app code.
  • tests/: Unit and integration tests.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Please follow these guidelines:

  1. Fork the repository and create a feature branch.
  2. Write tests for your changes.
  3. Run existing tests to ensure no regressions.
  4. Submit a pull request with a clear description of your changes.

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
To run the test suite:

pytest

docs.title: Building Documentation
docs.text:
To build the documentation locally:

mkdocs build

To serve the documentation locally:

mkdocs serve

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: DFVG can be installed in several ways depending on your needs: as a Python library, a standalone executable, or as part of a development workflow.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python: 3.8 or higher
  • FFmpeg: Required for transcoding. Ensure it is installed and available in your system PATH.

docs.title: Installation via pip
docs.text:
The easiest way to install DFVG is using pip:

pip install dfvg

docs.title: macOS (Homebrew)
docs.text: brew install ffmpeg

docs.title: Ubuntu/Debian
docs.text:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ffmpeg

docs.title: Windows
docs.text: Download the latest build from the official FFmpeg website and add the bin folder to your system PATH.

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
To install DFVG for development:

  1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/example/dfvg.git
cd dfvg

  2. Create a virtual environment (recommended): 

    python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate

  3. Install dependencies: 

    pip install -e .

  4. Install development dependencies (optional): 

    pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

docs.title: Mobile App
docs.text: The DFVG mobile app allows you to monitor and control jobs from your iOS or Android device.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Desktop App Running: Ensure the DFVG desktop app is running on your computer.
  • Same Network: Connect your mobile device to the same Wi-Fi network as your computer.

docs.title: iOS
docs.text:
1. Install Expo Go from the App Store.
  2. In the DFVG desktop app, navigate to the Mobile tab.
  3. Use your phone's camera to scan the displayed QR code.
  4. Tap the notification to open the app in Expo Go.

docs.title: Android
docs.text:
1. Install Expo Go from the Google Play Store.
  2. In the DFVG desktop app, navigate to the Mobile tab.
  3. Open Expo Go on your phone and tap Scan QR Code.
  4. Scan the QR code displayed in the desktop app.

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text:
• Status Overview: View active, completed, and failed jobs at a glance.
  • Quick Actions: Start common workflows with a single tap.

docs.title: Job Monitoring
docs.text:
• Detailed Progress: Track percentage complete, current file, and time remaining.
  • Notifications: Receive alerts when jobs complete or encounter errors.

docs.title: Remote Control
docs.text:
• Pause/Resume: Control active jobs remotely.
  • Cancel: Stop unwanted jobs.
  • Retry Failed Jobs: Quickly restart failed tasks.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Connection Issues: Ensure both devices are on the same Wi-Fi network. Check firewall settings on your computer.
  • QR Code Scanning: Make sure your phone's camera lens is clean and the QR code is clearly visible.

docs.title: Web Interface
docs.text: The DFVG web interface provides a user-friendly way to manage your transcoding jobs from any device on your local network.

docs.title: Accessing the Interface
docs.text:
1. Start the web server: 
    dfvg-web
  2. Open your browser and navigate to http://localhost:8000 (or the IP address displayed in the terminal if accessing from another device).

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The dashboard is your main control center. - Source Directory: Enter the path to your footage or use the "Browse" button (Electron only). - Scan: Detects all compatible video files (MP4, MOV, MKV) and extracts metadata. - Stats: View total duration, clip count, and color profile distribution. - Mode Selection: - Compact (H.265): Generates 720p proxies only. Best for quick drafts. - ProRes (HQ): Generates ProRes 422 HQ intermediates + proxies. Best for editing.

docs.title: Job Management
docs.text:
• Start New Job: Drag and drop files or select a directory.
  • Pause/Resume/Cancel: Control active jobs directly.
  • View Logs: Access detailed logs for troubleshooting.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
• Output Configuration: Set default output directories and file naming conventions.
  • Transcoding Profiles: Customize encoding settings (bitrate, resolution, codec).
  • Network Access: Enable/disable remote access and configure port settings.

docs.title: Mobile Optimization
docs.text: The web interface is fully responsive and optimized for mobile devices, allowing you to monitor jobs from your phone or tablet.

docs.title: Common Workflows
docs.text: DFVG is versatile. Here are the most common ways to use it.

docs.title: 1. The "Daily Dailies" Workflow
docs.text:
Goal: Review today's footage on your phone or laptop without filling up storage.

  1. Ingest: Plug in your SD card.
  2. Select Mode: Choose Compact (H.265).
  3. Process: DFVG will generate small, 720p proxies.
  4. Sync: Open the DFVG mobile app and scan the QR code on your desktop.
  5. Review: Watch clips smoothly over Wi-Fi, rate them, or download them to your phone for social media.

docs.title: 2. The Professional Edit
docs.text:
Goal: Prepare footage for a color-critical edit in DaVinci Resolve or Premiere.

  1. Ingest: Offload your footage to a fast SSD.
  2. Select Mode: Choose ProRes (HQ).
  3. Process:
    • DFVG transcodes your H.265/HEVC source files into ProRes 422 HQ.
    • It also creates low-res proxies.
  4. Edit:
    • Import the ProRes folder into your editor.
    • Enjoy buttery smooth scrubbing and playback, even with 5.3K footage.
    • Benefit from 10-bit color depth during grading.

docs.title: 3. The "Hybrid" Archive
docs.text:
Goal: Keep masters small but have edit-ready files for current projects.

  1. Shoot: Record in high-efficiency H.265 on your DJI Action.
  2. Archive: Store these original H.265 files on your long-term NAS/HDD.
  3. Active Project: When you want to edit a specific trip:
    • Run DFVG in ProRes Mode on just that folder.
    • Edit with the ProRes files.
    • Once the project is done, delete the ProRes files and keep only the original H.265 archive + project file. You can always regenerate the ProRes later!

docs.title: 4. Mobile Transfer
docs.text:
Goal: Get a clip from your camera to your phone instantly for Instagram/TikTok.

  1. Run: Start DFVG on your laptop/desktop.
  2. Connect: Open DFVG Mobile and connect.
  3. Browse: Navigate to the clip you want.
  4. Download: Hit "Save to Camera Roll". DFVG sends the proxy (fast) or the master (high quality) depending on your choice.
